About the Clavis Voynich

Welcome to the Clavis Voynich (last updated Apr. 15th, 2026).

This is part of my Repeatable Voynich site, please have a look at its main page to understand some of the conventions and the lexicon I normally use.

This page provides a side-by-side comparison of the original manuscript images alongside their transcriptions. The text is presented in two formats: first using Voynich Eva font to mimic the original script, and second using a custom transliterated format. This transliteration is meant to highlight patterns in the Voynich text, as resulting from my working notes.

  • The transliteration uses the Slot alphabet.
  • Pages are grouped in clusters. Pages not falling in any cluster are not shown. Notice that, as explained in Note 009, these clusters seems to reflect structural differences in Voynich words.
  • Only running text in paragraphs is shown (e.g. "labels" are omitted).
  • Words that contain unreadable characters are grayed out.
  • Unstructured words are shown in red.
  • Separable words are split into their two components, separated by an equal ('=') sign. For example, 'chockhy' can be seen as the combination of two regular words, 'cho' and 'ckhy'. Therefore, 'chockhy' shows as 'cho=ckhy' in the transliteration.
  • Interesting words are underlined.

This page has been generated automatically using ClavisVoynic.java.

Copyright Massimiliano Zattera.


This work is licensed under a Creative Commons Attribution-NonCommercial-ShareAlike 4.0 International License.

Images of the Cipher manuscript (Voynich manuscript). General Collection, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University, contained in these pages have been downloaded from Internet Archive and are assumed to be available for public use. Massimiliano Zattera, the publisher of this site, holds no rights on these images.

Cluster HA (Herbal A)
